steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence sets up a before/after contrast: the conjecture did something to mathematicians for 48 years, then finally ‘yielded’ to a proof. ‘Yielded’ implies resistance was overcome, so the blank needs a word meaning the conjecture blocked or defeated mathematicians.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'appeased': means satisfied or calmed — the opposite of what an unsolved problem does. ✗
  • Choice B — 'disregarded': means ignored. A conjecture can't ignore people. ✗
  • Choice C — 'confounded': means baffled or defeated. An unsolved conjecture confounds mathematicians trying to prove it — and then finally yielding means that resistance was overcome. ✓
  • Choice D — 'indoctrinated': means taught beliefs by repetition, irrelevant to mathematical difficulty. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
